mission.title: falco: Migrate Falco image from debian to distroless
mission.description: Migrate Falco image from debian to distroless. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current falco deployment
steps.description:
Verify your falco version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
helm list -n falco 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working falco installation.

steps.title: Review falco configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant falco configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get configmap -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=falco
```
**Motivation**

To reduce attack surface and patching maintenance, we should use the most minimal base image for the Falco image as possible.

Currently debian:11-slim is used, however Google's distroless image would be more lightweight.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Migrate Falco image from debian to distroless
steps.description:
Hey  , thanks for looking into this!

So, as you can see, there are two images for Falco one is the `no-driver` and the other is the self-contained Falco / `driver-loader` images. The two are very different and this issue is mostly related to the `no-driver` image _because it's so much easier to
```yaml
docker run --rm -i -t \
           --privileged \
           -v /var/run/docker.sock:/host/var/run/docker.sock \
           -v /proc:/host/proc:ro \
           falcosecurity/falco-no-driver:latest falco --modern-bpf
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get events -n falco --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Migrate Falco image from debian to distroless" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hey  , thanks for looking into this!

So, as you can see, there are two images for Falco one is the `no-driver` and the other is the self-contained Falco / `driver-loader` images. The two are very different and this issue is mostly related to the `no-driver` image _because it's so much easier to handle_ as you correctly identified.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with falco installed or the issue environment reproducible.
